name: Em Sherif
description:
About
Em Sherif is a renowned upscale restaurant in Beirut, known for its authentic Lebanese cuisine and fine-dining experience. Founded by self-taught chef Mireille Hayek in 2010, the restaurant aims to recreate the warmth and hospitality of traditional Lebanese households through its home-cooked meals. The ambiance is designed to resemble a luxurious Lebanese home, with antique-style decor creating a nostalgic atmosphere.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in an extraordinary culinary experience featuring authentic Lebanese cuisine with rich flavors and delicate presentations
2
Enjoy live music entertainment while savoring traditional Lebanese dishes like Kibbeh with yogurt and decadent desserts
3
Experience the luxurious ambiance resembling an antique oriental mansion for a classy dining atmosphere
reviews_summary: Em Sherif is a highly acclaimed Lebanese fine-dining restaurant in Beirut, known for its exquisite set menu offering a plethora of delicious and delicate dishes. Reviewers praise the scrumptious yet refined flavors of dishes like minced meat Rkakat, Sambousek, Kibbeh with yogurt, and a unique muffin-shaped puff pastry filled with rice and condiments. The ambiance is described as elegant and nostalgic, enhanced by live music performances. However, some reviewers caution about the large portions served in the set menu that may lead to overeating.
working_hours: Sunday: 9–1AM, 12–2AM | Monday: 12–1AM, 12PM–2AM | Tuesday: 12–2AM, 12PM–2AM | Wednesday: 12–2AM, 12PM–2AM | Thursday: 12–2AM, 12PM–2AM | Friday: 12–2AM, 12PM–2AM | Saturday: 12–2AM, 12PM–2AM
tips: Consider pacing yourself during the meal due to the generous portions served in the set menu | Visit on weekends to enjoy live music performances reflecting old Lebanese songs for added cultural immersion | Avoid sitting near areas where smoking (cigars, shisha) is allowed if you are sensitive to smoke
reviews:
[5/5] [May 18, 2025 from
Google]: The food is amazing and the service is very fast. Their set menu is amazing for groups and offers all kinds of lebanese dishes. || [5/5] [Sep 20, 2025 from
Google]: Em Sherif never disappoints, and its summer location at the Palm Beach Warwick Hotel is truly something special. With superb vibes and a breathtaking view over Beirut, it’s the perfect spot to savor the city in all its beauty. The ambiance is elegant yet inviting, with a stunning interior that elevates the whole experience. And of course, the food remains as exceptional as ever…rich, authentic, and full of flavor. A place that captures the spirit of Beirut at its finest. || [5/5] [Nov 18, 2025 from
Google]: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ If I could give 10 stars, I would. Em Sherif is, without exaggeration, the best food I have ever had in my life. I can’t even describe the experience properly, every bite feels like art. The flavors, the atmosphere, the service… everything is on another level. Even on a Monday night, it somehow turned into one of the best nights I’ve had. The energy, the attention to detail, the music, the dishes coming one after the other… unforgettable. In Lebanon we have Fairuz, Baalbek, the Cedars of God and Em Sherif (the 8th wonder of the world) If you’re in Beirut and you don’t eat here, you haven’t truly experienced Lebanon. || [4/5] [Jun 1, 2023 from
Google]: Food tastes good. But honestly speaking, the set menu is definitely overserved. The quantity is way too much for the number of personas it served, I happen to over hear the next table commented the same. Fine restaurants need to be conscious about the food waste instead of showing off. Besides, this restaurant doesn’t allow take away. It even makes it worse || [5/5] [Apr 22, 2022 from
Google]: You’re slowly biting into a piece of Sambousek covered in Hummus, with some Hindbeh leaves in the mix… You close your eyes to savor the moment as you let the explosion of flavors invade you. A nostalgic Fairuz song is playing in the background, and a soft breeze is caressing your cheeks. Life is good. It’s especially good at Em Sherif where the culinary experience combines richness and delicateness, comfort and elegance, past and present. A word of caution: you may have to roll out of the place, as walking would be physically impossible after what can only be described as a gargantuan feast… So you may want to pace yourself. It’s a set menu that takes the stress out of ordering, and ensures that energy is re-directed towards devouring the meal. And it’s classically Lebanese, with the cold and hot appetizers, the warm main dishes, and the desserts, all with small refined portions. Everything was delicious but if I were to pick some highlights: - The minced meat Rkakat and Sambousek were so scrumptious yet delicate at the same time - The Kibbeh with yogurt is another hit. The flavoring, the umami, the freshness of the yoghurt…combine magically together - Then when you think you’re done… comes a pleasant surprise: A muffin-shaped puff pastry filled with rice, minced meat, chicken and a whole array of condiments (raisins, almonds, shaved carrots, peas), sprinkled with a cinnamon flavored gravy… Sort of a “Riz aa Djej” with a twist. You won’t know what hit you. And you might come back for a second, third, maybe even a fourth serving. - The desserts.. what a beautiful note to end this extraordinary performance on. The date and molasses cake might trigger a few inappropriate sounds of approval. The Mhallabye with the loukoum and biscuits will feel decadently sinful. It’s a beautiful experience. Yes your tummy will beg you to stop, but your your heart and soul will beg for an Encore…

name: Liza
description:
About
Liza Soughayar, known for her eponymous Paris restaurant serving Lebanese cuisine, took a bold step by opening Liza Beirut in her home city. Housed in a renovated 19th-century mansion adorned with challenging artworks by young Lebanese artists, the restaurant features dining rooms with unique decor such as wallpaper replicating old Lebanese Pound banknotes and silhouettes of bombed Beirut buildings.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience comforting and fancy ambiance
2
Enjoy delicious food with great presentation
3
Explore a national treasure with beautiful architecture
reviews_summary: Liza is a renowned restaurant in Beirut known for its comforting and fancy ambiance, delicious food, and beautiful architecture. Reviewers praise the dessert selection, friendly staff, quick service, professional presentation of dishes, and the overall classy atmosphere. However, some mention small portions and high prices as drawbacks. The restaurant is highlighted for being a national treasure with stunning inside and outside architecture that makes it perfect for personal celebratory dinners.
menu_items: Fattoush, Kebab, Granola, Chorizo, Hummus, Spinach salad, Tabbouleh
working_hours: Sunday: 12PM–12AM | Monday: 12PM–12AM | Tuesday: 12PM–12AM | Wednesday: 12PM–12AM | Thursday: 12PM–12AM | Friday: 12PM–12AM | Saturday: 12PM–12AM
tips: Try their desserts if you have a sweet tooth | Consider valet parking due to limited parking spaces in the area | Avoid peak times or special events like weddings to ensure a more peaceful dining experience

Google]: We jumped right into the lively place without reservation and were welcomed! The place has a cool vibe, that's for sure, very well set up outdoors, cozy and quiet Food tastes fantastic, but portions are not generous! For me, it was a proper bite, or could've been better Kebabs were a bit overdone. We've been served bread once, and it was a tiny piece of bread The cotton candy desert was a little bit of a disappointment; overall, it was bland. I've tasted this dish in a way better taste and shape || [5/5] [Jun 21, 2025 from
Google]: I tasted arak for the first time at Liza’s ;) Here I found myself seated in the intimate courtyard of a historic homestead, jasmine flowering in front of me, a fountain splashing behind me and then this thrilling taste of subdued sweetness of an anise drink on a balmy spring day (1st May). Waiters in ‘Beyrouth je t’aime ’ tee shirts were graced with trays and platters bearing an array of dishes starting with a beautiful artichoke salad tossed with garden-fresh peas,coriander,spring onion :) and ending with a joyful rice and caramel pudding. In between many shared dishes with my friends,Lebanese-style hospitality,there was time to peek inside the alluring Liza mansion and buy one of those ‘je t’aime’ tee shirts to express my reaction to Beirut- a city that took me by surprise. Aside from communicating reviews,the effortless artistry and elegance at Liza’s and astounding authenticity of Lebanon at large,left me speechless. || [4/5] [Aug 12, 2025 from
Google]: A beautiful restaurant with stunning architecture. We visited during the day and the restaurant was empty, it might have a livelier and nicer atmosphere in the evenings. Overall the food was tasty, but unfortunately, the prices felt quite high for the value and quantity offered.

name: Mayrig
address: 282 Pasteur Street, Mansour Building, Beirut, Lebanon
description:
About
Mayrig is a renowned Armenian restaurant in Beirut, known for its traditional dishes made with fresh ingredients and silk road spices. The restaurant has gained popularity over the years, attracting both locals and expats. Despite being affected by the Beirut Blast, Mayrig was able to make a comeback with the help of crowd-funding. Its success has led to the opening of another branch in Cairo, Egypt.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Savor delicious Armenian cuisine with a unique twist on Lebanese favorites
2
Enjoy generous portions of flavorful dishes like kabab kerez and manti
3
Experience warm ambiance and attentive service in a cozy setting
reviews_summary: Mayrig offers a delightful dining experience with flavorful Armenian dishes and generous portions. Reviewers praise the delicious kabab kerez, manti, and Lebanese favorites like hummus and fattoush with an Armenian twist. The warm ambiance, attentive service, and friendly staff create a cozy atmosphere for enjoying homemade comfort food. While some mention that certain plates could be improved, overall Mayrig is highly recommended for its authentic flavors and welcoming hospitality.
working_hours: Sunday: 12PM–12AM | Monday: 12PM–12AM | Tuesday: 12PM–12AM | Wednesday: 12PM–12AM | Thursday: 12PM–12AM | Friday: 12PM–12AM | Saturday: 12PM–12AM
tips: Try the kabab kerez dish with a side of yogurt for an extra tasty experience | Ask the staff for food recommendations to discover outstanding dishes | Visit during off-peak hours to avoid long wait times due to busyness

name: Babel Bay
description:
About
Babel Bay is a chic and popular restaurant located in Beirut, Lebanon, known for its flavorful seafood dishes made with fresh ingredients. The interior is designed to be homey and attentive, featuring a small library, a wooden bar, and a study area. It's an ideal spot for couples looking for a romantic dining experience with delectable food in a private setting. The restaurant offers unique flavors and exceptional preparations that take seafood to the next level.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in high-quality seafood dishes with Lebanese twists
2
Experience impeccable service and warm hospitality
3
Enjoy complimentary desserts and fruits at the end of your meal
reviews_summary: Babel Bay is a high-end restaurant in Beirut's Zaytouna Bay known for its impeccable service, delicious seafood dishes, and complimentary desserts. Reviewers praise the friendly staff, cozy yet stylish atmosphere, and beautifully presented plates bursting with flavor. While some mention minor disappointments with certain dishes like vine leaves or raw fish platter, overall recommendations highlight the exceptional quality of food like shrimps fattet and white fish raw. The restaurant's attention to detail in presentation and service make it a top choice for tourists seeking a luxurious dining experience.

The achta ice cream was good, and while the fruit presentation was appealing, the taste didn’t quite match. Highly recommended for seafood lovers! || [5/5] [Oct 18, 2025 from

Google]: I'm not a fish person but at this restaurant I had the best fish experience of my life. I was entertained every time and the dessert they serve is very tasty and gives a nice finishing touch after the meal. || [4/5] [Aug 1, 2025 from
Google]: Babel is constant and consistent. The entrees are alone are absolutely delicious way before you get to start with your choice of fresh fish. Yummy from start to finish; consistently! || [5/5] [Jun 10, 2025 from
Google]: Babel Bay always delivers a polished and memorable dining experience. From the moment we walked in, the attention to detail was evident. We asked about a menu item that wasn’t available, and the way the hostess explained the alternative reminded me why this place stands out—staff are clearly well-trained, familiar with every detail of the menu, and genuinely focused on the guest experience. The food, as always, was delicious. Even on a public holiday, the level of service didn’t drop—a rare thing in Beirut. And the small touches, like the presentation of the hot towels, continue to reflect the elegance and consistency that Babel is known for. If I may offer a gentle suggestion or two: • For raw fish dishes, a bit of ice underneath would help keep things perfectly fresh—especially during warmer weather. • And for the bread service, maybe consider placing the hot bread separately from any plastic-wrapped items, just to maintain that premium feel and avoid unnecessary contact with heat and packaging. That said, Babel Bay remains one of the few places where you feel every element is carefully thought through—and that’s why it continues to be a favorite.

name: Tawlet
description:
About
Nestled in the vibrant Armenian quarter of Bourj Hammoud, Tawlet is a culinary gem that embodies the spirit of Lebanese cuisine. Founded by Kamal Mouzawak as part of the Souk el Tayeb initiative, this restaurant connects city dwellers with local farmers, showcasing fresh produce in a delightful buffet format. Each day at Tawlet features a guest chef from various villages across Lebanon, offering diners an authentic taste of regional specialties.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience diverse regional variations of Lebanese cuisine prepared by guest chefs from different villages daily
2
Enjoy fresh ingredients sourced directly from small-scale farmers at Souk el Tayeb market
3
Partake in cooking classes to delve deeper into Lebanon's culinary culture
reviews_summary: Tawlet in Beirut is a standout restaurant praised for its delicious and healthy Lebanese cuisine. Reviewers rave about the exceptional talent of chef Laure Ghosn, highlighting dishes like batata mechwiyé, warak enab b zeit, and sambousik bé loz w Joz. The freshness and flawless execution of every item impress diners, with mentions of outstanding salads, roast beef in pomegranate sauce, and traditional Lebanese desserts. Guests appreciate the cozy atmosphere, varied buffet lunch with modern twists on traditional dishes, and attentive service from the executive chef.
working_hours: N/A
tips: Visit during lunchtime to enjoy the all-you-can-eat buffet featuring a variety of traditional Lebanese dishes with modern twists | Try the lemonade with rose water as a refreshing addition to your meal | Engage with the chefs who are happy to explain each dish's background and offer insights into local cuisine | Consider joining cooking classes offered at Tawlet to learn more about Lebanese culinary traditions

name: Le Sushi Bar
address: New Starco Building, Rizkallah Street Minet El-Hosn Sector, Beirut, Lebanon
description:
About
Le Sushi Bar, established in 1998 by Mario Haddad, is a pioneer of sushi in the Middle East. It introduced popular dishes like crazy maki and volcano maki, now widely replicated. The restaurant won the Restaurant & Bar Design Awards in 2020 for its stunning interiors by Gregory Gatserelia. Despite being pricier than other options, patrons believe that the excellence of Japanese cuisine justifies the cost.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience exceptional sushi dishes with high-quality ingredients
2
Enjoy an intimate and elegant ambiance ideal for special occasions or date nights
3
Receive top-notch service from friendly and knowledgeable staff members
reviews_summary: Le Sushi Bar in Beirut is a must-visit for sushi lovers seeking an exceptional dining experience. The ambiance is intimate and elegant, perfect for a date night. The staff are friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable, ensuring impeccable service. Dishes like marinated salmon, seared scallops, crab salad, and black cod are highly recommended for their exquisite taste and presentation. While the prices are on the higher side, the quality of food and service justifies the cost. Overall, Le Sushi Bar offers outstanding sushi in a refined setting that guarantees a memorable dining experience.
menu_items: Dessert, Sashimi, Tataki, Seafood, Tempura, Edamame, Sushi

name: Barbar-Hamra
address: VFWH+68C، PIccadilly Street, Hamra, Beirut, Lebanon
description:
About
Barbar-Hamra is a beloved spot in Beirut, celebrated for its comforting and straightforward Lebanese fast food that won’t break the bank. This eatery offers an impressive selection of grilled meats, fried delights, and vegetarian options alongside tempting desserts. The crunchy falafels and stuffed shawarmas have garnered a loyal following among locals and visitors alike. Many rave about the tender beef shawarma, which may be small but packs a flavorful punch with its decadent tahini sauce.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience authentic Lebanese street food at an iconic 24/7 restaurant
2
Enjoy flavorful dishes such as chicken shawarma sandwiches and grape leaves
3
Try the best falafel in town at affordable prices
reviews_summary: Barbar-Hamra in Beirut, Lebanon, is a popular 24/7 restaurant known for its great reputation among locals for taste and volume. The atmosphere is described as uplifting and cosy, with friendly service from young staff. While the prices are reasonable considering Lebanon's high price standard, the flavor of dishes like the chicken shawarma sandwich is highlighted. Some reviewers mention fast service, affordable prices, and delicious food options like falafel and shawarma sandwiches.

name: M. Sahyoun Falafel
description:
About
M. Sahyoun Falafel is a renowned establishment known for serving the ultimate falafel sandwiches, with a secret recipe that dates back to 1935. The shop is part of a pair of adjacent establishments owned by two brothers who are no longer on speaking terms, each offering similar menus and sparking debates about which one is superior. Visitors can expect tasty falafel, reasonable prices, and a welcoming atmosphere at this classic hole-in-the-wall spot in Beirut.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience authentic and flavorful falafel sandwiches at reasonable prices
2
Witness the hands-on approach of the dedicated owner wrapping sandwiches with precision
3
Enjoy generous portions served alongside fresh pita bread, vibrant salads, and creamy tahini sauce
reviews_summary: M. Sahyoun Falafel is a must-visit spot in Beirut, Lebanon, known for its authentic and flavorful falafel sandwiches at reasonable prices. The owner's hands-on approach and dedication to quality make this place stand out among the rest. Visitors rave about the crispy yet soft falafel, fresh pita bread, vibrant salads, and creamy tahini sauce that create a burst of traditional flavors with each bite. The generous portions and budget-friendly prices cater to both long-time falafel enthusiasts and first-time visitors alike. With a rich history dating back to 1935, M. Sahyoun continues to impress locals and tourists with its delicious offerings.
working_hours: Sunday: Closed | Monday: 9AM–10PM | Tuesday: 9AM–10PM | Wednesday: 9AM–10PM | Thursday: 9AM–10PM | Friday: 9AM–10PM | Saturday: 9AM–10PM

name: Ferdinand
description:
About
Ferdinand is a European pub located in Beirut, Lebanon. It has received 369 reviews and offers a menu with a range of prices. The restaurant is listed as one of the best spots for French brasseries and bistros in Lebanon. It's known for its relaxed and trendy environment, serving delicious dishes at affordable prices. Additionally, it's praised for its great service and laid-back atmosphere, making it a popular choice among visitors to Lebanon.

The place offers a variety of local and small batch alcohols to try, making it a must-visit spot for those looking for quality drinks and pub food.

name: Onno Bistro
description:
About
Onno Bistro is a Lebanese-Armenian fusion restaurant that offers a rich culinary experience. The menu features dishes like cracked bulgur wheat with chili, kibbeh of raw lamb and lentils, boreg filled with sheep's milk cheese, hummus, stuffed grape leaves, and batata harra. With generous portion sizes and reasonable prices, this restaurant has become a culinary landmark in Bourj Hammoud.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Savor authentic Lebanese-Armenian fusion cuisine bursting with flavors like Bulgur Salad (Itch) and ground beef Manti.
2
Enjoy top-notch service in a cozy atmosphere that adds to the culinary adventure.
3
Indulge in delicious desserts served generously after your meal.
reviews_summary: Onno Bistro is a hidden gem offering a fusion of Lebanese and Armenian cuisine, where guests rave about the delicious Bulgur Salad (Itch), ground beef Manti, kofte yogurt soup (Madzounov), lahmajun, and chocolate pistachio cake. Despite limited parking and close quarters seating with smoking allowed, the restaurant's friendly service and top-notch dessert selection make it worth the visit. The menu bursts with mouthwatering dishes prepared with fresh ingredients in a lean and delicious way, making Onno Bistro a go-to spot for an unforgettable dining experience.

About

The menu features an array of starters and mains, including traditional Lebanese dips, meats, and vegetarian options. With its extensive vegan menu, the restaurant caters to diverse dietary preferences. Local patrons frequent this hidden gem for its mouthwatering Lebanese cuisine and inviting ambiance.

name: BARON
description:
About
Baron, located in Beirut, is a European pub that offers a relaxed and trendy environment with beautifully plated dishes at an affordable price. Founded in 2016 by chef Athanasios Kargatzidis, also known as Tommy, the restaurant focuses on showcasing local produce and features an ever-changing fine-dining menu based on market availability and fresh catches.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience a chic and cozy ambiance ideal for a date night or special occasion
2
Try exceptional dishes like the Soujouk Dates, Calamari, and Feta that stand out for their flavors
3
Explore an original menu that offers unique twists on traditional Lebanese cuisine
reviews_summary: Baron is a chic and cozy restaurant with a great ambiance, perfect for a date night or fancy gathering. While some dishes like the Soujouk Dates, Calamari, and Feta were exceptional, others fell short in terms of taste and portion size. The service is friendly and attentive, but the high prices may not always be justified by the overall dining experience. Despite mixed reviews on certain dishes, Baron stands out for its original menu showcasing Lebanese flavors in unique ways.
menu_items: Green curry, Ice cream, Duck confit, Ramen, Quinoa, Brussels Sprouts, Banana split, Sesame, Bagel, Steak

name: Jai asian Restaurant
description:
About
Jai Asian Restaurant in Beirut offers a relaxed and stylish setting with contemporary furniture. The menu features a variety of spicy Indian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes such as chicken, shrimp rolls, fish cakes, and noodle soups seasoned with coriander, cumin, ginger, or curry. The open kitchen creates a tranquil atmosphere where patrons can enjoy high-quality food prepared by Chef Wael Lazkani.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience Arab chefs preparing Indian and Thai cuisine for a unique twist on familiar dishes
2
Enjoy delicious food with standout items like pad Thai noodles and butter chicken
3
Savor an authentic holiday-like ambiance at this small yet charming restaurant
reviews_summary: Jai Asian Restaurant in Beirut offers a unique dining experience with its cozy and intimate setting, where seating is limited but the food quality shines. The chefs, predominantly Arabs, bring a distinct touch to the Indian and Thai cuisine served. While some dishes like the PHO soup may miss the mark, others like the pad Thai noodles and butter chicken impress diners. The ambiance exudes holiday vibes, complemented by friendly staff and affordable prices. Complimentary desserts add a sweet touch to an already delightful meal.
menu_items: Steamed dumplings, Kombucha, Chicken curry, Beef, Pakora, Larb, Green curry, Sesame chicken, Spring roll, Soup, Curry, Noodle, Bulgogi
working_hours: Sunday: Closed | Monday: 11AM–11PM | Tuesday: 11AM–11PM | Wednesday: 11AM–7PM | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 11AM–11PM | Saturday: 11AM–11PM

What we liked is that it wasn’t greasy and on the heavy side. We had: Prawn dumplings which I thought lacked a bit of prawns on the inside. The coconut and lemongrass soup was the highlight of our meal. The best we had! The spinach & peanuts salad was amazing. The butter chicken light and tasty, but in my view could take a bit more chicken. We also had coconut korma curry w/ shrimp which was very nice. We felt the shrimps were too small and too little. We also had amazing Chapati bread. Make sure you order it ahead, it’s freshly made and will take abt 10min. Overall a great experience. About
Al Falamanki Raouche offers a delightful Lebanese brunch experience every Saturday, featuring a buffet of traditional Lebanese dishes such as hummus, fattoush, foul, kebbe and manaouche. The restaurant's traditional decor and beautiful ceramic tiles create an atmosphere that celebrates Lebanese culture. Guests can enjoy shisha and play backgammon while savoring Arabic delicacies.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Enjoy delicious Lebanese cuisine made from fresh ingredients
2
Experience breathtaking sea views while dining
3
Family-friendly atmosphere suitable for all diners
reviews_summary: Al Falamanki Raouche offers a delightful dining experience with stunning sea views overlooking the iconic Raouche Rock. The food is described as delicious, prepared with fresh and high-quality ingredients, showcasing traditional Lebanese cuisine. Guests particularly enjoyed dishes like tabbouleh, ice cream with kashta, labneh, foul, manakish, and more. The ambiance is praised for being family-friendly and welcoming for all diners. While some found the prices to be on the higher side, the excellent service provided by friendly staff members like Jad enhanced the overall experience.

Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Lebanese flavors in a relaxing setting. ☕🍳🌿 || [5/5] [Oct 19, 2025 from
Google]: Perched above the iconic Raouché rock, Al Falamanki delivers one of the most breathtaking views in Beirut. Whether you’re a local or visiting for the first time, this Lebanese café restaurant captures the city’s soul, effortlessly blending nostalgia, comfort, and a sense of place that few spots can match. The setting feels both authentic and unpretentious: vintage Lebanese décor, rustic furniture, and greenery that wraps around the terrace, giving the place a warm, lived-in charm. It’s not fancy or sophisticated, but that’s exactly its beauty. Every corner feels curated with heart, a tribute to Beirut’s golden days. The menu stays true to traditional Lebanese favorites, from fresh manakish straight off the saj to creamy labneh, perfectly grilled halloumi, and aromatic foul mdammas. Everything tastes homemade, served with generosity and a smile. The staff are attentive and friendly, moving at a relaxed Beirut pace, enough to make you linger, enjoy the food, and soak in the view. And that view is simply unforgettable. Overlooking the Mediterranean and the legendary Pigeon Rocks, it’s one of the most stunning breakfast backdrops you can find anywhere in the city. Morning hours are especially magical, with soft light, calm air, and the sound of waves below. Al Falamanki Raouché is more than just a restaurant; it’s a Beirut experience. Perfect for a leisurely breakfast, a shisha with friends, or a sunset tea, it’s a place that captures everything that makes this city so irresistibly charming. ⭐ Highly recommended, especially for breakfast with a view you’ll never forget. || [1/5] [Dec 22, 2025 from
Google]: Do not recommend this place. We (5 adults) visited Falamanki on 20th Dec 2025 - we tried booking a table online and via their website - non existent. Yet when we arrived to the restaurant (4:30pm), we were treated poorly due to not having a booking. We waited roughly 20 mins for a table (not an issue - as we understand restaurants get busy at this time). After being seated - we ordered dinner & Shisha, (mix of starters, mains and drinks), the food came within a reasonable time (25 mins). Throughout the evening, the Shisha we ordered was regularly topped up by a gentlemen - and we tipped him fairly. At the end - we asked for the bill (photo below), having read the menu (also photo below) - it clearly specifies that ‘prices are in USD and include service and VAT’. Our bill was $163.5. We left $165 in the cup, and when the waiter came, he threw the additional $1 in our face and said ‘ma 7arzene - khodoha’ (i.e its not worth it - keep it), and then he stormed off. Horrible behaviour. A tip is up to the discretion of a customer, and shouldn’t be looked down upon by the waiter. The menu clearly outlines that service is included in the ‘inflated’ menu prices. After 5 minutes - the waiter came back to explain his abrupt behavioural outburst - and mentioned that the service charge on the menu does not go to the waiters, but rather to the restaurant itself. This is an issue - but one that waiters should raise with management, and not take out on the customers. We ended up leaving a 5 dollar tip to the waiter - but very disappointed with how our evening ended. For a restaurant to be charging over $30 dollars a person, and not have a functional way of booking a table is absurd. || [4/5] [Aug 7, 2025 from

description:
About
Cocteau Restaurant is a world-renowned French restaurant located in the heart of Beirut. The restaurant offers a wide range of dishes, including steaks and salads. The service is excellent and the atmosphere elegant. The prices are on the higher side, but it is worth it for the quality of food.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in superb French cuisine including fresh crab salad and exquisite bouillabaisse
2
Experience exceptional hospitality from Pierre Freije for a truly unforgettable dining experience
3
Enjoy a romantic evening or business entertainment in the chic retro interiors of Cocteau
reviews_summary: Cocteau Restaurant in Beirut is highly praised for its exceptional food quality and service. Reviewers highlight the delicious salmon tartar, perfectly grilled steak, and rich pain perdu. The ambiance is charming with excellent decor, while Pierre Freije's hospitality stands out. Despite being on the pricey side, Cocteau offers a delightful dining experience with top-notch cleanliness, taste of food, menu variety, service, and atmosphere.
working_hours: N/A
tips: Make reservations in advance to secure a table at this popular upscale restaurant | Visit during winter for hearty dishes like bouillabaisse that are well-suited for the season | Consider trying classic dishes as well as innovative items inspired by local ingredients on their ever-changing menu
reviews:
[5/5] [Feb 15, 2024 from
Google]: On valentine night we decided to try cocteau for romantic dinner. We ordered salmon tartar was super delicious and fresh. Then as a main dish i tried coeur de fillet-steak the meat was super delicious and the way they grilled the meat is extraordinary. We ended this dinner with pain perdu so rich. Highly recommended once you are in beirut it’s a little bit expensive but definitely worth it. Thank you cocteau for this amazing night👏 || [2/5] [Feb 15, 2025 from
Google]: Cocteau wasn’t that impressive. The food quality didn’t meet expectations, with the escalope being too oily, and the overall ambiance wasn’t great. Service felt intrusive, which made the experience uncomfortable. Could have been better. || [5/5] [Oct 3, 2025 from
Google]: We celebrated our one-year anniversary here and it was perfect. The cake I ordered was presented beautifully, the manager was welcoming and attentive, and the food was outstanding. The atmosphere made the night unforgettable an amazing experience all around! || [5/5] [Apr 19, 2025 from
Google]: This restaurant holds a traditional, iconic status, but unfortunately, it relies more on its name and location than the quality of its food. I hadn’t been in years and was genuinely disappointed when I went back. The food was below average across the board. The beef stroganoff, which used to be my favorite dish here, was terrible this time — and the salad and dessert weren’t any better. In fact, the dessert was the worst part of the meal. To make matters worse, both my friend and I felt sick after dining there. It’s a shame to see a place with such a legacy lose its touch. || [4/5] [Oct 20, 2025 from
Google]: Nice atmosphere and good service food is tasty but small portions

name: Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh
description:
About
Nestled in the heart of Beirut, Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh offers an authentic Italian dining experience. The trattoria boasts a menu filled with mouth-watering antipasti, pizzas, pastas, and experimental dishes that blend classic Mediterranean flavors. The rustic interior exudes coziness and is adorned with paintings of the Tuscan countryside.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience authentic Italian cuisine in a cozy setting
2
Try the impressive Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and delicious pizza
3
Enjoy warm hospitality from friendly and attentive staff
reviews_summary: Appetito Trattoria Gemmayzeh is a cozy Italian restaurant with a warm and inviting atmosphere. Reviewers praise the delicious food, particularly highlighting the Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and pizza. The staff is friendly and attentive, although some mention slight service delays. The decor creates an authentic Italian feel, perfect for romantic dates or small gatherings. While there were minor issues like missing utensils in one review, overall, guests highly recommend this place for its tasty dishes and top-notch service.
menu_items: Pizza, Caprese salad, Spaghetti, Lasagna, Tagliatelle
working_hours: N/A
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid potential service delays when the restaurant is fully booked | Don't miss trying the Gnocchi Alla Siciliana and thin-crust pizzas which are highly recommended by reviewers | Engage with the staff who are described as polite and accommodating to enhance your dining experience | Take part in unique experiences like spinning the wheel game at the entrance for a chance to win free items from the menu
reviews:
[5/5] [Dec 9, 2023 from
Google]: I couldn't wait till I get home and give a rating!!!! Superrr clean !!!! Super delicious!!!!! Great team and polite staff. Simply in love with this place ❤️ It deserves 100/10 !!! Highly recommended 🤩🤩 || [4/5] [Aug 12, 2019 from
Google]: The Italian decor and warm feel take one on an Italian journey. The music is nice. As for the food it not only looks good it tastes just the same. The Gnocchi Alla Siciliana is truly impressive you have to try it if you find yourself in this cozy restaurant. || [4/5] [Feb 19, 2022 from
Google]: Love the new location! Delicious pizza dough! Melanzane was good, Tiramisu was okay. Great service! the place was fully booked, so guys literaly had to run between tables. They definetely need more staff. || [4/5] [Dec 18, 2019 from
Google]: If you need to get the Italian taste in Lebanon, this place is your go to one! It’s a good place for a date/ small gathering! The hospitality/ service/ food quality were amazing! I just didn’t like few small things about the service: the table missed some forks and napkins... the bill took a bit long to arrive (even-though the place wasn’t full) || [3/5] [Dec 1, 2017 from
Google]: What started out as a comfortable late lunch ended with the stench of Gorgonzola, literally. We happily went to Appetito Trattoria because they cater to our youngsters by providing high chairs. The service was slow and towards the end some platters really took their time in arriving to our table. I would like to mention that the waiter was running back and forth between tables, until he was later joined by more staff. We ordered the Siciliana and Caprese salads, the Prosciutto e Funghi pizza, the Gnocchi alla Sorrentina, the Linguine al Pesto, and the Ravioli di Capra. I will leave the encounter with the salads till the end. I would like to begin with the pizza; it was delicious. The crust was thin and crispy, and I really enjoyed the flavors. I think that if you are craving Italian pizza, this would definitely do. We ended up ordering another pizza because it was the best thing we had eaten at Appetito. The Linguine al Pesto was good and I was glad that the pasta was cooked al dente. Also, the ravioli was good, but paled in comparison to the pizza. Now back to the salads, my sister had found an unwanted visitor in her Caprese and we failed to taste the Gorgonzola in the Siciliana salad dressing (which was the thing going for it to differentiate it from a regular salad). It was appreciated that the manager really listened to our comments, and that he showed us the Gorgonzola cheese that had been used. However, it was a bit too much when he then showed us how it becomes when they process it in the mixer, and made us smell it before and after. Then, he explained how he had to change to this Italian Gorgonzola with no taste and a faint smell because customers before thought that the Gorgonzola was too strong, and it went on and on. For a change of taste and smell, we opted to try their Soffice al Cioccolato; it was alright (they were kindly offered on the house). Although I would recommend Appetito Trattoria if you are in the mood for pizza in a cozy environment, the only thing that was on my mind leaving this place was Gorgonzola.

name: BUCO
description:
About
BUCO in Batroun offers a luxurious and laid-back dining experience with stunning views of the coast. The restaurant is known for its rustic elegance and inviting ambiance. While it's popular for its burgers, the menu also features a variety of appetizers perfect for sharing. Reservations are recommended due to its popularity. The restaurant in Saifi on Gouraud street is often busy, so booking ahead is advisable.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience trendy dining in a beautiful setting in Beirut
2
Try the standout Mulberry Bacon burger with unique flavor combinations
3
Enjoy juicy and flavorful steaks at Buco
reviews_summary: Buco in Beirut offers a trendy and stylish dining space with solid food options, particularly highlighted by their juicy and flavorful steaks. The burgers are popular but opinions vary on them. The restaurant's ambience is praised for being perfect for a relaxed meal in the heart of the city. One standout burger is the Mulberry Bacon, known for its fresh and juicy Australian grain-fed meat, crispy onion crunch, and unique mulberry jam that balances sweet-salty flavors perfectly. The staff receives high praise for their service quality.
menu_items: Patty melt, Wagyu beef, Lobster roll, Hamburger, French fries, Slider
working_hours: N/A
tips: Make reservations to ensure seating preference as there have been issues reported with outdoor table bookings | For burger enthusiasts, don't miss trying the Mulberry Bacon burger known for its balanced sweet-salty flavors from mulberry jam | Visit during off-peak hours to avoid noise and crowds indoors if looking for a quieter experience | Engage with friendly staff like Hadi who enhance the overall dining experience
reviews:
[5/5] [Oct 11, 2025 from
Google]: Great service, amazing food! Highly recommended! Must try the lobster rolls, and the wagyu burger 🍔 😋 better to reserve || [4/5] [Jul 8, 2025 from
Google]: Buco offers a beautiful dining space with elegant interior design and a trendy vibe , a great spot for a night out in Beirut. The food overall was good. The steak was juicy and flavorful, definitely the highlight of the meal. The burger, while decent, wasn’t the best I’ve had. Still, everything was nicely presented and the service was smooth. Prices lean on the higher side compared to the overall taste, but if you’re looking for a stylish setting and solid food, Buco is worth a visit. || [4/5] [Nov 8, 2025 from

Overall, the food and atmosphere are good, but better handling of reservations would improve the experience. || [4/5] [Jun 27, 2025 from

Google]: Buco, Beirut. Absolutely killer fries, a wagyu burger that easily climbs on the Lebanon podium, and a team as dynamic in the kitchen as on the floor. Packed Sunday lunch, almost zero false notes. 🍔🔥

name: Al Falamanki Sodeco
description:
About
Al Falamanki Sodeco is a relaxed and intimate restaurant located in the heart of Beirut. It offers a traditional style and laid-back vibe, making it the perfect place for a midday meal after exploring Downtown Beirut. The restaurant is known for its fine dining experience and sophisticated Levantine dishes, providing an ideal setting for dining under the stars.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Enjoy Lebanese dishes in a charming setting that captures the essence of old Beirut
2
Picturesque outdoor seating surrounded by lush greenery
3
Rich selection of flavorful Lebanese favorites including kebabs and mezze
reviews_summary: Al Falamanki Sodeco in Beirut is a gem of a restaurant that exudes the charm and spirit of old Beirut. The setting is picturesque, perfect for both day and night dining, where patrons can enjoy beautifully arranged outdoor seating surrounded by lush greenery. The menu offers a rich selection of Lebanese favorites, from juicy kebabs to an assortment of mezze, each dish more flavorful than the last. The staff's hospitality is commendable, always attentive and quick to provide recommendations. Al Falamanki is not just a place to eat but a cultural experience, ideal for family gatherings or a relaxed evening with friends.

name: Fred Kitchen & Bar
description:
About
Nestled just off the bustling main street of Gemmayzeh, Fred Kitchen & Bar is a delightful Franco-Italian eatery brought to life by the talented chef Alfred Asseily from Ecole Ritz Escoffier. This charming restaurant and bar exudes warmth with its lamp-lit tables and lush greenery, creating an inviting atmosphere perfect for any occasion. The menu boasts a tantalizing array of Mediterranean dishes, including their signature goats cheese salad and the renowned Croque de Fred.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience amazing food quality and presentation
2
Enjoy a charming and cozy setting with different dining rooms
3
Try delicious dishes like tuna tartare, roquefort endives salad, burger, magret de canard
reviews_summary: Fred Kitchen & Bar in Beirut is a beloved spot for many diners, with patrons praising the amazing food quality and presentation, attentive yet unobtrusive service, charming and cozy setting with different dining rooms, and a diverse menu offering delicious dishes like tuna tartare, roquefort endives salad, burger, magret de canard, pain perdu, and fondant. The atmosphere is enjoyable while the wine list is diverse. Chef Alfred's expertise shines through in creating an excellent Parisian bistro experience that exceeds expectations.
menu_items: French fries, Risotto, Spaghetti, Bloody Mary, Ceviche, Roast chicken, Negroni
working_hours: Sunday: 12–3:30PM | Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 12PM–12AM | Wednesday: 12PM–12AM | Thursday: 12PM–12AM | Friday: 12PM–12AM | Saturday: 12PM–12AM
tips: Check for specials on the menu for unique offerings | Visit during Saturday lunch for a quieter ambiance | Don't miss trying the pain perdu or fondant desserts | Consider asking for recommendations from staff like Nadeem or Joe for an enhanced experience

Surprised to see negative reviews, our experience was really good actually, glad there’s such a good Parisian style resto in Beirut. Google]: Pleasant experience at Fred. The service was good from booking the table to the service at the restaurant. The food quality is up to the expectations and the taste is fine. The wine list is diverse and they also have a nice cocktail bar. || [5/5] [Apr 7, 2024 from
Google]: Definitely my new most favourite spot in Beirut. Didn't realise it was the same restaurant by chef Alfred who used to be close to Sofil so I felt like rekindling with an old friend. name: Bayt Em Nazih
address: VFWP+5GM, Urban Gardens bldg Abdul Baki st right turn off Hamra main street before "Kababji, Beirut, Lebanon
description:
About
Bayt Em Nazih is a Lebanese restaurant located in Hamra, offering authentic cuisine and a spacious environment for work meetings or study groups. During the day, it provides a relaxed and trendy atmosphere with beautifully plated dishes at affordable prices. The cafe caters to various dietary needs including vegetarian, gluten-free, Halal, and vegan options. While it's great for intimate dates and group gatherings surrounded by greenery, visitors should be aware that the night ambiance transforms into an old local music scene.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in traditional Lebanese breakfast
2
Enjoy authentic Lebanese cuisine at reasonable prices
3
Experience a unique dining setting with self-service concept
reviews_summary: Bayt Em Nazih is a charming Lebanese restaurant known for its authentic cuisine, cozy atmosphere, and reasonable prices. Guests appreciate the self-service concept, short waiting times, and the opportunity to play backgammon while enjoying their meal. The food quality is exceptional with dishes like Tabbouleh, Hummus, Falafel, and mixed kebabs receiving high praise. The staff are friendly and welcoming despite the lack of table service. Additionally, live music events on weekends add to the fantastic ambiance of this hidden gem in Beirut.
working_hours: N/A

name: Kampaï
description:
About
Kampaï is a modern Japanese restaurant located in Downtown Beirut, offering an extensive menu of Japanese cuisine including appetizers, soups, salads, tempura, and various sushi options. The restaurant's Zen decor provides a relaxing ambiance for guests to enjoy their meal at any time of the day. Known for its attention to detail and commitment to quality, Kampaï offers an upscale dining experience with luxurious sushi offerings.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience consistent quality Japanese cuisine
2
Enjoy delicious dishes like crispy spicy salmon and eel sushi
3
Relish in a cozy atmosphere with professional staff
reviews_summary: Kampaï is a Japanese restaurant in Beirut known for its consistent quality and delicious food, although some reviewers find the portions small for the price. The service is described as efficient but not overly friendly, with a mix of opinions on the atmosphere. Customers praise dishes like crispy spicy salmon, ceviche, crispy crab salad, white shrimp sushi, and eel sushi. While some note a decline in portion sizes and service quality over time, others appreciate the cozy atmosphere and excellent staff.

name: Loris Restaurant
description:
About
Loris Restaurant in Gemmayze, Lebanon is a culinary gem that offers a delightful dining experience blending traditional Lebanese flavors with modern cooking techniques. The restaurant, located within the elegant Le Gray Beirut, provides stunning city views and an inviting atmosphere. Loris boasts a 7-page menu featuring an extensive array of Lebanese dishes such as hummus, fatouch, batata harra, fetet betenjen, and knefeh.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience traditional Lebanese dishes with arak
2
Enjoy delicious mezze options such as makanek and wara2 enab
3
Appreciate the cozy atmosphere ideal for socializing
reviews_summary: Loris Restaurant offers a mix of reviews, with some praising the delicious food options like makanek and wara2 enab, the cozy vibe perfect for gatherings with family or friends, and the attentive staff members like Ahmad Istanbuli. However, there are contrasting opinions mentioning disappointing experiences with certain dishes like tabbouleh and homos, as well as issues with service quality. Overall, Loris seems to provide a pleasant dining experience for those looking to enjoy Lebanese-Armenian cuisine in a beautiful setting.
menu_items: Lahmacun, Batata harra, Fattoush, Kebab, Mochi, Falafel, Pita, Hummus, Fish and chips, Grape leaves

name: Tavolina
description:
About
Nestled in a charming little street, Tavolina is an inviting trattoria that captures the essence of traditional Italian cuisine. With a menu brimming with delectable wood-fired pizzas, rich pastas, and hearty risottos, it’s a haven for food lovers. The ambiance is cozy and laid-back, perfect for enjoying meals either indoors or outdoors.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1

2

3

reviews_summary: Tavolina is a highly recommended Italian restaurant known for its exceptional food with rich, authentic flavors that transport diners to Italy. name: Swiss Butter
description:
About
Nestled in a charming locale, Swiss Butter is a delightful French cuisine restaurant that exudes a rustic charm with its high ceilings and dim lighting, creating an inviting atmosphere perfect for any dining experience. Known for its mouthwatering steaks and signature sauces, the menu features delectable options like Beef Fillet and their renowned Swiss Butter Burger. Guests can enjoy generous portions beautifully presented alongside house wines and an array of beers. The ambiance is lively yet cozy, making it a popular spot on weekends.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Unique concept of a steakhouse with a twist
2
Delicious meals served with signature sauce
3
Affordable prices compared to portion size
reviews_summary: Swiss Butter in Gemmayze is a new concept steakhouse offering only three main dishes - chicken, meat, or salmon - all served with their signature sauce, green salad, fries (or baked potato), and bread. Reviewers praise the delicious food at affordable prices and commend the friendly staff despite the crowded nature of the restaurant. The cozy atmosphere, smoke-free environment, and quick service are highlighted by visitors. While some mention small protein portions, others rave about the beef fillet cooked to perfection and recommend adding chili to enhance flavors. Desserts like pain perdu receive high praise for their taste. Overall, Swiss Butter stands out for its tasty meals but may have limited options for vegetarians/vegans.

The moment you step inside, you'll be greeted with warmth and hospitality that makes you feel like part of the family. The chefs here are true masters of their craft, offering mouthwatering suggestions that will elevate your dining experience to new heights.

name: The Shogun Lounge

About

Their menu features an array of delectable items such as the Temaki available in small and large sizes, boasting crunchy nori leaf and fresh salmon skin. Another standout dish is the Hamashi, known for its freshness and complementing flavors of thinly sliced lemons.

name: Beit Kanz
description:
About
Beit Kanz is a charmingly renovated Lebanese home in Beirut that offers not only delicious culinary experiences but also supports underprivileged families across Lebanon. The establishment hosts a Christmas Fair where visitors can purchase exquisite products, contributing to the community. Managed by NGO Beit el Baraka, Beit Kanz is more than just a restaurant; it's a cultural hub featuring a terroir shop, artisanal boutique, and cafe-restaurant. This space provides an immersive journey into Lebanon's heritage and craftsmanship.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience an exclusive oriental ambiance blending elegance and warmth
2
Enjoy delicious Lebanese food rich in flavor and beautifully presented
3
Immerse yourself in a cozy atmosphere reminiscent of a beautiful Lebanese home
reviews_summary: Beit Kanz is a place that evokes a feeling of home and captures the essence of Beirut in its most beautiful form. The ambiance is described as elegant, charming, and full of character with traditional decor that makes it feel like stepping into a Lebanese home. While some reviewers mention smaller portions and higher prices, others praise the exceptional flavors of authentic Lebanese dishes served with care. The staff are highlighted for their kindness and attentiveness, adding to the warm atmosphere. Despite mixed reviews on specific dishes, Beit Kanz stands out as a hidden gem in Beirut known for its unique setting and memorable dining experience.
working_hours: Sunday: 9AM–11:30PM | Monday: 9AM–11:30PM | Tuesday: 9AM–11:30PM | Wednesday: 9AM–11:30PM | Thursday: 9AM–11:30PM | Friday: 9AM–11:30PM | Saturday: 9AM–11:30PM
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid noise from few tables being taken | Try the chicken dish which stands out among other menu items | Don't miss trying the meghli eclair or molasses fondant for dessert | Explore their shop for high-quality zaatar
reviews:
[5/5] [May 18, 2025 from
Google]: Beit Kanz is one of those rare places I find myself returning to over and over again—not just for the food, but for the feeling it evokes every time I walk through its doors. It’s more than a restaurant; it’s a home. A sanctuary that captures the essence of Beirut in its most beautiful, soulful form. Nestled in the iconic Tabbal building in Ashrafieh, Beit Kanz is a story of resilience and beauty. After the devastating port blast in 2020, the building was restored with such care and love that it now stands as a testament to Beirut’s ability to heal and shine again. The renovation preserved its architectural soul, and stepping inside feels like being embraced by Beirut’s rich heritage. Every visit is a journey. From the warm service to the comforting aromas of authentic Lebanese dishes, everything is crafted with intention and heart. The food is simply exceptional—flavors that remind you of home, prepared with generosity and served with genuine care. And what makes the experience even more meaningful is the spirit behind it: Beit Kanz is powered by Beit El Baraka, a foundation dedicated to supporting the community, which gives your meal a deeper sense of purpose. The staff are not only professional but deeply kind and attentive, making every guest feel like a long-lost friend. Whether you’re seated indoors, surrounded by vintage Beiruti charm, or enjoying the fresh air in the garden, you’re transported to a Beirut that feels timeless. Beit Kanz is more than a dining spot. It’s a reminder of who we are, where we come from, and how even in difficult times, beauty, tradition, and kindness continue to thrive. || [3/5] [Apr 19, 2025 from
Google]: I was really looking forward to trying this place, especially given its stunning location. Tucked away in the heart of Achrafieh, the setting is genuinely beautiful whether you’re seated inside or outside , a true hidden gem in terms of atmosphere. Unfortunately the food didn’t live up to the surroundings. We ordered a wide selection of dishes, but overall it was below average. The only standout was the chicken, which was well-prepared and flavorful. The sweets at the end also managed to leave a good impression. Thanks god It’s a shame because with such a charming spot, you’d expect the food to match. I truly hope they work on improving the menu because the potential is there. || [5/5] [Oct 24, 2025 from
Google]: Absolutely loved Beit Kanz! The food is fresh, delicious, and beautifully presented. The place has such a warm and cozy vibe, and the two little boutique shops inside are super charming , perfect for a quick browse after your meal. Great service and amazing atmosphere. || [5/5] [Dec 3, 2025 from
Google]: Beit Kanz is the kind of place you would go to try a very unique food fusion. The concept is Lebanese traditional food with a twist. Honestly I wasn’t expecting to like the food as much given there’s a lot of restaurants that offer Lebanese cuisine with similar concepts. Oh boy i was surprised, we started with the beetroot carpaccio with goat cheese and honestly it was my first time trying something like this. It’s a mix of sweet from the molasses and sour from the goat cheese and the walnut add a deep earthy flavor while the zaatar gives it the freshness. The other dish that was the highlight was the halloumi dumplings with tomato sauce. Upon seeing this item i was really intrigued to try it. I’m not going to spoil it because i really want you, reading this review, to try it out as i believe it’s a must have. Overall the experience was really good and the team working there were very professional and helpful. I would definitely recommend visiting during this holiday season to get the chance to see the Christmas decoration. || [5/5] [Jun 14, 2025 from
Google]: From the moment we arrived at Kanz Restaurant, we were immersed in a truly exclusive oriental experience. The ambiance is a perfect blend of elegance and warmth, with both indoor and outdoor seating options that make it ideal for any occasion. The food? Absolutely delicious , every dish was rich in flavor, beautifully presented, and cooked to perfection. Whether you're a fan of traditional tastes or looking to explore something new, Kanz delivers on every level. What made the visit even more special was the friendly and attentive service. The staff, especially Zahi, went above and beyond to ensure we felt welcomed and well taken care of. Kanz isn’t just a meal, it’s a memorable dining experience. Highly recommended!

name: COUQLEY

About
Couqley French Bistro, established in 2010, is a renowned dining spot in Beirut. It has been recognized as one of the top French bistros in Lebanon. The menu offers a variety of delectable dishes, with the steak frites being a standout favorite. Other must-try items include the duck confit and an array of healthy salads.

reviews_summary: Couqley is a charming French restaurant known for its cozy atmosphere and delicious food, particularly the steak frites. Reviewers praise the attentive service, quality of meat, flavorful dishes like poulet frites and pain perdu dessert, and the overall ambiance. Some mention a slight decline in food quality but still recommend it for gatherings with friends or special occasions.

name: KAMI Experience

About
KAMI Experience is a vibrant urban bar that artfully blends innovative Japanese fusion cuisine with a stylish, street-inspired aesthetic. The chic interior, designed in collaboration with Brazilian graffiti artist Felipe Yung, sets the stage for an unforgettable dining adventure focused on bold flavors. Ideal for sharing, KAMI's menu features delightful small plates like tempura avocado adorned with bonito flakes and salmon tartare served on crispy rice squares.

description:
About
Indigo on the Roof, perched atop the luxurious Le Gray Hotel in Beirut, is a dining gem that promises an unforgettable experience. This sophisticated restaurant offers two delightful settings: dine indoors with ample sunlight and stunning views or bask in the fresh air on the outdoor terrace while soaking up breathtaking cityscapes. Whether you're indulging in breakfast, enjoying a leisurely Sunday brunch, or savoring dinner under the stars, Indigo on the Roof caters to all occasions.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience consistent 5-star treatment
2
Enjoy calm and beautiful rooftop ambiance
3
Try delicious dishes like homemade chips with labne dip and crispy duck salad
reviews_summary: Indigo on the Roof at Le Gray Hotel in Beirut offers a consistent 5-star treatment with calm and beautiful rooftop ambiance. Reviewers appreciate the versatile menu, attentive service, and delicious dishes like homemade chips with labne dip, salmon tiradito, and crispy duck salad. The setting under the stars creates a cozy and romantic atmosphere for an enjoyable dining experience.

tips: For a more intimate experience, visit during weekdays or early evenings to avoid crowds | Try replacing avocado with artichokes in the salmon tiradito for a unique twist on flavors | Don't miss out on trying the Crispy Duck Salad as it's described as a pleasant surprise by reviewers | Consider ordering cocktails from the bar next door if you prefer them over wine from their list
reviews:
[5/5] [Jul 28, 2017 from
Google]: It is nice to feel how consistent all Le gray outlets are, between Gordon's cafe and indigo you can make sure that you are receiving 5-star treatment:) Indigo's rooftop is very calm, beautiful setting and classy service. The menu is versatile but could use the addition of salads options. Welcome of the servers is appreciated and their attention enhances the experience. The home made chips and crackers with the labne dip are to die for! The bread baguette is equally tasty! I tried the salmon tiradito where i replaced the avocado with artichokes, the sauce tangy and sweet, i loved it! the wild mushroom risotto was not super but we could sense the quality of the ingredients used. The finale was the pain perdu which was ok, but not to dance for, something lacking in the texture. Overall the experience is to be repeated, nice view and enjoyable ambiance. || [4/5] [Oct 26, 2017 from
Google]: Indigo was a last minute decision, but it was exactly what we needed at that moment. After a long marathon-like day, we were looking for a place to just relax. Situated at the top of the fancy Le Gray Hotel in Beirut, Indigo provided an outdoor seating area underneath the stars. Dim lights created a cozy and romantic atmosphere, granted they also resulted in the reduced quality of my photographs. The lower part of the view was obstructed by pretty flowers and vines, but the upper part of tall structures managed to make it up to this restaurant perched on the roof. The waiters guided us to our table, and when they were present, the service was great. They were attentive, polite, and very professional. However, at times it was a bit difficult to get a hold of them. My significant other ordered wine from the wine list, but I felt like having a cocktail, which they readily made available from the bar next door. I should add that the Strawberry Mojito was really good and refreshing. The waiter came to us with a bread basket in hand, from which we chose the olive and multi-cereal pieces of bread. They were soft and delicious, especially after they were smeared with the complementary rich butter. We ordered the Crispy Duck Salad and Ahi Poke as appetizers. The crispy duck salad was a pleasant surprise, the crunchy bits of the crispy duck were a perfect addition of texture to the salad. As for the ahi poke, it was very flavorful and the sauce complemented the tuna well. For mains, we ordered the Thai Baked Sea Bass in Banana Leaf and the Black Angus Tenderloin. The sea bass baked in the banana leaf was as moist as it could be; it was extremely tender. Unfortunately, the accompanying rice was forgotten until we reminded the kitchen. However, the waiter was quick to apologize and did end up bringing the rice to our table. Moving on to the steak, it was a good chunk of meat, but nothing outstanding. To satisfy our sweet tooth, we ordered the Valrhona Chocolate Fondant and the Namelaka, which consisted of chocolate mousse, hazelnut biscuit, and passion fruit ice cream. Needless to say that everything becomes better with chocolate, and these were two solid chocolate desserts. The warm chocolate inside the fondant was a great way to end the meal. In general, Indigo has good food, mostly great service, and an intimate setting, with nothing separating you from the sky above. || [5/5] [Apr 4, 2019 from
Google]: Amazing location, excellent food and superior service. One of Beirut's absolute top restaurants. || [5/5] [Jul 20, 2018 from
Google]: Great staff, delicious, wonderful ambiance, lovely dinner on the roof with a breathtaking magnificent view || [4/5] [Nov 23, 2017 from
Google]: Amazing rooftop with a lovely view Great food and drinks. Rating criteria: Cleanliness 5* Taste of food 5* Menu Variety 5* Service 5* Smoking Price 5* Friendly employees 5* Atmosphere 5* Music NR Cocktails NR Special needs 5* Kids NR

Mezyan, located in the vibrant district of Hamra, is a hidden gem offering a delightful culinary experience. name: Tabliyit Massaad
description:
About
Tabliyit Massaad, also known as Massaad Express, may seem like a typical fast food joint at first glance, but it offers much more. The chain is known for its vibrant atmosphere and affordable prices. What sets it apart are the exceptional sandwiches served on wooden boards called Tabliye. These sandwiches come with unexpected touches like toasted sesame seeds and generous portions that leave you satisfied.

Reviewers highlight the friendly service, quick delivery, cozy atmosphere, and affordable prices. The menu offers a fusion twist on traditional Lebanese cuisine with options like potato, hummus, cauliflower sandwiches, saje burgers, chicken wings with pomegranate sauce, and more. While some dishes like grilled potatoes received mixed reviews, the overall experience is highly recommended by visitors who appreciate the excellent service and tasty food.

name: Salon Beyrouth

Salon Beyrouth, located in the heart of Hamra, is a stylish retro-bar that has been serving as a hub for Beirut's thriving jazz community for almost a decade. The venue offers an extensive selection of over 120 whiskies from around the world and craft classic cocktails with homemade bitters and infusions. Its elegant art-deco-inspired interior and covered alfresco garden area provide the perfect setting for intimate gatherings or lively evenings of live music.

reviews_summary: Salon Beyrouth is a beloved spot in Lebanon, known for its flawless execution of food with standout dishes like fish and chips, mushroom risotto, prawn risotto, burger, Japanese cheesecake, and pain perdu. Albergo is a grand all-suite hotel located in the Achrafieh neighborhood of Beirut. Housed in a 1930s mansion, this luxurious hotel is part of the prestigious Relais and Chateaux series. The ochre-colored ancient villa with a large gilded cast iron gate draped in greenery sets the scene for an elegant stay. The hotel's interior combines French glamour with unique Lebanese touches, creating a characterful and romantic ambiance.

name: Amelia

About
Amelia Beirut is a high-end and selective restaurant located in Mar Mikhael, offering a delightful cuisine and a unique ambiance. The interior design combines Lebanese traditions with modern style, creating an exquisite atmosphere for gathering with friends. Reservation is highly recommended due to its popularity. The menu features must-try items such as shrimp halloumi rolls, truffle pizza, and shrimp barrel. name: Ritage Garden
description:
About
Nestled in the heart of Beirut on Lebanon Street, Ritage Garden is a culinary gem that has been delighting diners with authentic Lebanese cuisine since 2015. This charming restaurant offers an extensive menu filled with starters, mezze, main courses, and desserts crafted from fresh, locally-sourced ingredients. The inviting ambiance makes it an ideal setting for special occasions like proposals or leisurely afternoons spent with loved ones.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Experience impeccable service and knowledgeable staff in a sophisticated ambiance
2
Enjoy fresh, well-prepared dishes bursting with exquisite flavors
3
Savor culinary masterpieces with balanced textures and refined tastes
reviews_summary: Ritage Garden offers a sophisticated ambiance with impeccable service and knowledgeable staff. The food is described as fresh, well-prepared, and bursting with exquisite flavors. Dishes are culinary masterpieces with a nice balance of textures and refined flavors. The outdoor space is praised for its great atmosphere, while the desserts like chocolate fondant are highlighted for being delicious. Ritage by Maroun Chedid stands out as one of Lebanon's most refined dining experiences, combining world-class international cuisine with unmatched elegance in an iconic setting at Tabet Palace.
working_hours: Sunday: Closed | Monday: 12:30PM–12AM | Tuesday: 12:30PM–12AM | Wednesday: 12:30PM–12AM | Thursday: 12:30PM–12AM | Friday: 12:30PM–12AM | Saturday: 12:30PM–12AM
tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and enjoy a more intimate dining experience | Try unique dishes that span Italian, Japanese, Lebanese, Armenian cuisines for a diverse culinary experience | Make reservations in advance to secure a table at this popular spot | Explore the cocktail list for perfectly complementing drinks like the bell pepper smash or Moscow mule
reviews:
[5/5] [May 13, 2025 from
Google]: Ritage by Maroun Chedid continues to stand tall as one of Lebanon’s most refined and memorable dining experiences. Helmed by the brilliant chef himself, the restaurant has carved a distinctive reputation for combining world-class international cuisine with unmatched elegance and charm. Set within the iconic Tabet Palace, Ritage offers a setting that is as captivating as the food, effortlessly blending historic grandeur with contemporary flair. It’s the kind of place you fall in love with at first sight and find yourself coming back to, again and again. On my most recent visit—a Sunday buffet—I was reminded of why Ritage is in a league of its own. The spread was nothing short of magnificent, featuring a rich and diverse array of dishes that spanned Italian, Japanese, Lebanese, and even Armenian cuisines. Every plate was a testament to quality, flavor, and creativity…succulent, balanced, and deeply satisfying. For over eight years, Ritage has remained a benchmark of excellence on Lebanon’s culinary map. The impeccable service, stunning atmosphere, and bold yet refined flavors make it more than just a restaurant, it’s an experience, and one I’ll always be eager to relive. || [5/5] [Jul 12, 2025 from
Google]: What a lovely time we had in this restaurant! The garden setting is so nicely done and makes the experience all the more enjoyable. Additionally, the sushi station was not replenished after a certain time, which was disappointing given the caliber of the buffet. To their credit, when I raised my concerns, management went over and beyond to respond—offering a poke bowl as a kind gesture. While it wasn’t what I was hoping for, the effort was appreciated. About

As you stroll along Alexander Fleming Street, you'll find Internazionale buzzing with well-dressed thirtysomethings enjoying cocktails and conversation outside.

It's described as a hidden gem with lovely welcoming staff that takes you to the roots of Lebanese life.

name: Paul
description:
About
Paul Bakery, a renowned French bakery chain, has carved out a special place in Lebanon's culinary scene. Known for its delectable array of freshly baked goods, Paul offers an impressive selection of artisanal breads like classic baguettes and unique varieties such as olive and walnut bread. This charming bakery is a must-visit for anyone eager to indulge in traditional baking at its finest.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in famous croissants and pastries at a renowned French bakery chain
2
Experience a cozy ambiance with indoor seating and an outdoor garden option
3
Enjoy high-quality bread, including artisanal options like olive bread and walnut bread
reviews_summary: Paul is a popular destination in Beirut known for its cozy ambiance, delicious pastries, and quality food. Customers appreciate the off-menu bresaola salad as a hidden gem and enjoy the consistency of their offerings across branches. While some mention slow service at times, the beautiful interior, high bread quality, and premium coffee add to the overall experience. The bakery's croissants are particularly praised for being buttery and flaky. Despite slightly higher prices and smaller portions due to its central location, visitors find Paul worth visiting for a casual meal or quick coffee break.

Google]: Great place with different indoor and outdoor dining options. Nice ambiance bistro type atmosphere. Service was a bit slow but the food made up for it || [5/5] [Jul 24, 2025 from
Google]: Everything about this place is so good — the ambience, the food, and the service. The food portions are generous and satisfying. The tiramisu looks amazing, though the taste doesn’t quite match its appearance, but it’s still not bad overall || [5/5] [Jul 10, 2024 from
Google]: Really enjoyed coming here this morning! Found it very relaxing and the food was great! So far I have enjoyed the consistency of their food in the several branches that I visited so far! Enjoyed having some coffee, a cheese croissant and also some sweet pastries! Thank you! || [4/5] [Jul 20, 2025 from
Google]: If you’re wandering through the charming streets of Gemmayzeh and craving a sweet pause, Paul is a cozy, reliable spot that blends French flair with a Beirut vibe. I stopped by for a quiet afternoon treat and tried their pain perdu, which came beautifully presented with a generous drizzle of caramel. Texture-wise, it was soft and warm on the inside with just the right amount of crisp on the outside. Sweet, but not overly so. The portion was satisfying, perfect for sharing or indulging solo with a coffee. I would have loved a touch more richness in the custard soak — hence the 4/5 — but overall, a very solid dessert and a lovely experience. Service was friendly and professional, and the ambiance had that classic Paul elegance. Whether you’re a local or a tourist, it’s a nice little stop for a refined sugar fix. 📍 Worth a visit if you’re in the area — especially if you like your French toast with a Parisian twist. || [5/5] [Oct 26, 2025 from
Google]: Paul Café in Gemmayze is such a charming spot.The croissants are always fresh, the coffee is rich and smooth, and the staff make you feel genuinely welcome. Whether you’re meeting friends or just need a quiet moment, it’s the perfect place to unwind in the heart of Beirut.

name: ASH Beirut
description:
About
ASH Beirut is a charming restaurant nestled in the heart of Beirut, offering a contemporary take on traditional Lebanese dishes. The menu features an array of appetizing starters and mouthwatering main courses, all expertly prepared to delight your palate. The ambiance is perfect for a romantic evening or intimate gathering with friends. Guests rave about the flavorful grilled calamari and juicy beef teriyaki, as well as the impeccable presentation of each dish.
why_to_go:
Why you should go
1
Indulge in exquisite traditional Lebanese cuisine with a modern twist
2
Experience exceptional meat dishes like duck confit and dry-aged prime ribs
3
Witness Chef Eddy Diab's careful food preparation in an open kitchen setting
reviews_summary: ASH Beirut is a highly recommended restaurant in the heart of Beirut, offering exceptional dining experiences. Reviewers praise the meat dishes, especially the duck and prime ribs, as well as the attention to detail in food presentation. The open kitchen concept allows guests to witness Chef Eddy Diab's meticulous preparation of each dish. The ambiance is cozy and romantic, perfect for a date night or small group gathering. Guests appreciate the warm welcome from staff and the creative touches throughout the restaurant, such as handles shaped like cutlery in the bathrooms.
