tips: For a memorable experience, try dishes like grilled calamari, red quinoa salad, risotto with mushrooms, and unique desserts prepared by Chef Eddy | If you prefer attentive service and generous portions of delicious cuisine, ASH Beirut is worth its slightly higher cost | Visit during weekdays for a quieter dining experience or on weekends for livelier atmosphere | Consider making reservations in advance to secure your table at this popular restaurant
reviews:
[5/5] [Jan 5, 2019 from
Google]: If you are looking to eat great food prepared with passion and love, then this is the place!! Cozy and Romantic! Perfect for a date night or small group. The Chef Eddy does an amazing job, his staff are extremely friendly and kind. Amazing food, absolute must visit!! Highly recommend the duck, it was cooked perfectly and the presentation was beautiful. The Chef preps all the food with care and attention. By definition, all restaurants should be run the same way. || [3/5] [Dec 21, 2019 from
Google]: Slightly disappointed. Went there for a date and was expecting a fine dining experience. The restaurant is located within an old building and finishing works and decorations at some areas seemed cheap compared to the price of the place. In as much as food is concerned, we ordered two of the chef creations, the pain perdu of duck confits and fruit chutney and the chicken beklava with brie cheese and dry beach. These were amazing! This appreciation for the chef's craft was quickly let down by the meat platters that followed. The black angus beef and melted short ribs that we ordered had weird spices and did not meet our expectations. NB: this review is for the a la carte experience and not the new visual mapping concept with formula similar to the one of le petit chef. || [4/5] [Aug 12, 2019 from
Google]: They take the open kitchen concept to an entirely new level. As you walk in you see Chef Eddy Diab in the kitchen preparing and carefully decorating the plates. The staff is very friendly and helpful, as for the food nothing can be said besides that it was a mouthwatering memorable experience. || [5/5] [Aug 1, 2017 from
Google]: Work discretely, behind the scenes, no huss or fuss about your project, keep it a secret like a pregnant lady during her first three months,and then, when you are ready,only then, throw the bombastic fantastic news! This is how I imagine Salt was designed by Chef Eddy! On a backstreet of Monot, a discrete sign will show you the way. Snow covered mountains on which the word Salt is crafted. And just there, in the center, a door that invites you inside where you will be carried away on a journey of discovery, teasing your palate and your senses. Let me start by saying that it was a happy birthday to me, that will be detailed in a post by itself ,  but what made the difference is the sweetness of Chef Eddy who opened the restaurant on a Monday evening just to grant me my wish. Eddy, I can never thank you enough. And the sweetness and good vibes are spread in the restaurant, from the floor manager to the waiters. The house was restored in a way to make it fresh with hues of blue and white, inviting to comfort you to stay and enjoy your meal. A sneak peek on the chef working his creation behind the counter and a window giving on the kitchen. A large wooden table spread with bread, jars and cooking books. And a bar in one corner of the ground floor. Climb few stairs, another ambiance even warmer like a living room with couches and a balcony with rocky rough walls softened by lights and tables. This could be perfect to be booked for large group to have a private dinner in a space dedicated just for them. Attention to details goes even to the bathrooms, handles shaped as cutlery: spoons, forks and knives! Creativity at its best! Moving on to what matters, Fooood;) The bread basket; warm as the welcome, with comforting butter and balsamic for those who want. We ordered: Onion soup: big heartwarming bowl of soup and two cheese toasts: balanced flavors, a great way to start a meal on a rainy eve. Salmon tartare; over a bed of lentils, decorated with slices of fresh tuna, and a special piece of makdous: a burst of flavor in your mouth! Red quinoa and shrimps salad: generously served and devoured! Grilled calamari and again, working on marrying the flavors is the key word for this dinner. Risotto, a creamy, chewy and with chunks of mushrooms, loved it;) oh and do not forget the Parmesan tulle crowning the dish! Not your regular meat balls We do shredded entrecote meat balls oven cooked and a side of fries.Speechless! I picked the title carefully;) yes Salt became Sweet! Chef Eddy spoiled the birthday girl indeed: The "Surprise du chef":  a dome of chocolate melting before you eyes with pouring hot chocolate and there the surprise awaiting you: strawberries on a mountain of rice pudding. Chef you are an artist! My birthday cake was cannelloni filled with white chocolate  and a sweet wish I would have never imagined a regular dinner could turn out like that. Thank you infinitely and I will be coming back to explore the menu hiding its treasures and watching the art of cooking properly done by a master! Again, without Salt food is tasteless;) || [2/5] [Jul 8, 2019 from
Google]: I was really disappointed. The place had very good reviews and high rating, and i had a lot of expectations. First, the ambiance was great. Staff was friendly and helpful. However they had a major watering problem. Each time we wanted to order we had to wait 10 min or more for someone to show up, and when we asked for the bill we waited alot eventhough there was only 4 tables including us. We ordered slow cooked cheek, smoked angus beef and beklawa chiken. Beklawa chicken and slow cooked cheek was good, however the angus was not. I ordered medium, it was over cooked and not tasty. I felt that the food is overpriced no value for money.
